enhancedCapabilities: The primary README now gives first-time APEX developers a plain-language path from product purpose to one-click installation, community context, API Key onboarding, and first use.
The API Key guide keeps real community screenshots and makes the short apexcn -apikey command the default setup path with clear placeholder and secret-handling guidance.
Twenty copy-ready natural-language prompts now cover common search, learning, drafting, publishing, reply, favorite, subscription, and diagnostic scenarios.
Release v0.80.6 was installed from the public latest endpoint and verified for checksum enforcement, version reporting, quoted and unquoted API Key setup, and 0600 config permissions.
unexpectedProblems: The first local asset checksum command ran from the repository root even though checksums.txt contains paths relative to artifacts, so the files were not found; rerunning inside artifacts passed all checks.
rootCauses: The release checksum manifest intentionally contains asset basenames, making its verification working-directory-sensitive.
preventionActions: Run shasum verification from the artifacts directory whenever validating the generated checksums.txt file.
Keep README onboarding contracts covered by documentation consistency and installer tests.
Continue public latest-endpoint installation smoke tests for releases that change installation or onboarding documentation.
nextMilestoneGoal: Keep roadmap milestone 0.8 active and its remaining Windows lifecycle, fresh independent novice validation, and seven-day readonly soak work paused until the user explicitly resumes it.
expectedResults: New APEX developers can understand apexcn-cli and complete the documented installation and API Key setup without reading developer internals.
The public latest release resolves to v0.80.6 with all required release and checksum assets.
No community API write, API Key generation, Windows lifecycle round, independent validation round, or soak run is performed by this documentation patch.
majorRisks: Community navigation labels and screenshots may drift as the live APEX application evolves and should be rechecked during future onboarding refreshes.
Real Windows lifecycle qualification remains paused and is not claimed by this patch.
Roadmap 0.8 core acceptance criteria remain pending while independent validation and soak gates are paused.

instructions: Re-read current repository state before planning.
Create only a just-in-time plan for the active milestone.
Resume the existing milestone; do not activate a later milestone until its acceptance and completion review are closed.
